headline: How France's pre-deportation centers became an 'extension of the prison system'
description: The rate of deportation for undocumented foreigners held in administrative detention centers has never been so low, even as detention periods have grown longer and conditions have deteriorated.

Trump tightens grip on Republican Party in US primaries
description: Candidates backed by Donald Trump for the Republican Party nomination in the November midterm elections benefit from the mobilization of the MAGA base, which remains loyal to the billionaire despite his declining popularity.

headline: NGOs sue French state over failure to tackle 'forever chemicals'
description: NGOs and residents living near contaminated sites filed legal action against the state to remedy the 'ecological harm' caused by its 'culpable inaction.'

headline: These winemakers believe melody makes better wine: 'Right after bottling, we play Beethoven on a loop'
description: In the vineyards, while fermenting or even during aging, some winemakers use various melodies that they believe stimulate the vines, protect them from disease or intensify the wine's aromas.

headline: From Italy to Libya, the dehumanizing processing of migrants
description: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (4/4). Exploited, extorted and abused in Libya, exiles who survive the crossing are rendered invisible and sometimes fall into the hands of mafias linked to agricultural businesses. But 'what matters is that irregular arrivals decrease,' as an official in Rome put it.

'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique
description: Six young French nationals were convicted in Paris for planning to migrate to Mozambique together and join the IS-affiliated jihadist group al-Shabab in Cabo Delgado province, where it has been active since 2017.

headline: ShinyHunters, the elusive hacking network that started in France
description: Despite multiple arrests, numerous data breaches continue to be claimed under the ShinyHunters banner. Beyond its French roots, the group has now become a cybercriminal 'brand.'

headline: Cuba's crackdown on dissidents continues, despite US pressure
description: In an effort to show good faith to Washington, Havana recently pardoned more than 2,000 detainees. However, most of those who benefited were ordinary delinquents. The Cuban authorities continue to arrest dissidents, bringing the number of political prisoners to 1,260, a record high.
